description: Scenario 3: Staple product BUT with explicit instructions and inducement — expected: possible_infringement

patent_context.patent_title: Method for enhanced concrete curing using polymer additives
patent_context.claim_description: A method of curing concrete involving the application of a specific polymer additive at controlled temperatures

product.product_description: A polymer additive commonly used in construction for various concrete treatment purposes. Widely available from multiple suppliers.
product.staple_reasoning: Polymer additives for concrete are widely sold commodity products with many uses beyond the patented method

supplier_conduct.reason_to_believe_details: Supplier was sent a cease-and-desist letter identifying the patent and explaining the infringing use. Supplier continued sales.
supplier_conduct.instructions_details: Supplier's product data sheet includes a specific section titled 'Enhanced Curing Method' that replicates the patented process step by step
supplier_conduct.inducement_details: Supplier ran trade magazine advertisements promoting their product specifically for the enhanced curing method described in the patent

evidence_flags.evidence_notes: Cease-and-desist letter on file; advertising materials and product data sheet available
